role: Listing venues and electronic order books that match buyers and sellers and sell market data; themselves listed companies.
scale: NYSE ~$31T, Nasdaq ~$32T+ listed cap (2025); US market ~$69T total

role: Provide liquidity by continuously quoting two-sided prices; capture bid-ask spread; dominate retail wholesale via PFOF.
examples: Citadel Securities, Virtu, Jane Street, Susquehanna
scale: Citadel Securities ~35% of US retail equity/options flow, ~25% of all US equities volume

role: Execute client orders (broker) and trade own inventory (dealer); underwrite and intermediate.
examples: Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, JPMorgan, Schwab, Robinhood

role: Pool retail and institutional capital into funds; dominant force in passive indexing.
Manage retirement assets for public and private employees; long-horizon institutional allocators.
scale: ~$29.8T total US; CalPERS $556B (largest US public plan)

role: Invest general-account assets (backing policy liabilities), heavily in fixed income and private credit.
examples: MetLife, Prudential, New York Life, Athene

role: Lightly constrained pools using leverage, shorting, and derivatives to seek absolute returns.
State-owned investment pools deploying surpluses across global public and private markets.
examples: Norway GPFG, ADIA, GIC, Saudi PIF

role: Individuals investing directly via brokers; flow largely routed to wholesalers via PFOF.
scale: Tens of trillions in household equity/fund holdings

role: Post-trade plumbing: clears, nets, settles (T+1), and custodies US securities; systemic infrastructure.
scale: $3.79 quadrillion processed in 2024; $100T+ assets under custody (2025)

role: Set and enforce rules across securities, derivatives, banking, and broker conduct.
scale: SEC oversees ~$100T+ in markets; FINRA polices 3,184 broker-dealers

role: Build and maintain rules-based benchmarks and the GICS sector taxonomy that funds track.
examples: S&P Dow Jones Indices, MSCI, FTSE Russell, Nasdaq
scale: S&P 500 underpins ~$10T+ in indexed assets; GICS = 11 sectors / 163 sub-industries
